rationale: The citing paper uses Dagogo-Jack and Forehand (2018) in its core theory section to support the claim that consumers use the self as a reference point in product judgments. It also cites this work when arguing that low self-brand connection reduces reliance on self-referencing, which helps justify the predicted moderation pattern. These citations are tied to hypothesis development about product identification and efficacy, so the reference plays a meaningful conceptual role rather than a passing background mention.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Dagogo-Jack and Forehand (2018) as conceptual support for self-referencing in brand/product evaluation and for the relevance of self-brand connection to that process. It builds its sharing-out mechanism on this base to explain reduced product identification and lower perceived efficacy.
